name: Pumpkin Vermont Spice Cake
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 325°F. Grease and flour two 9-inch round baking pans.
In a large bowl, cream together the granulated sugar and 3/4 cup of softened butter until light and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in the pumpkin puree and vanilla extract. Mix in the evaporated milk and water.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, and ginger until combined. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the pumpkin mixture, stirring just until incorporated.
Divide the batter evenly between the prepared pans. Bake in the preheated oven for 35 to 40 minutes, or until a wooden pick inserted into the center comes out clean. Cool on wire racks for 15 minutes before removing from the pans and allowing to cool completely.
For frosting:
In a large bowl, beat together the softened cream cheese and 1/3 cup of softened butter until smooth. Stir in the powdered sugar and maple flavoring until well combined.
Once the cakes have cooled, frost between layers and on top of the cake. For a two-layer cake, also frost between the layers over the sides of the cake. Garnish with the chopped nuts.
Debbie Holland, Feb. 1994
Found in St. Louis Southwest County Journal
RECIPE CLUB OCTOBER 1994
